data.title: Software firms need a ‘Google moment’ to prove their relevance amid AI disruptions – analyst
data.description:
Gene Munster, managing partner at Deepwater Asset Management, believes companies facing AI disruption must aggressively respond or risk losing their market positions.

In an interview with CNBC, Munster compared the current market tension to Google’s (GOOGL), (GOOG) successful defense of its search business last year, arguing that software firms (IGPT), (XSW), (IGV) in particular need a similar “Google moment” to prove their continued relevance.

The market has seen meaningful pullbacks across multiple industries this week, driven in part by a broader “risk off” sentiment among investors.

Munster noted that investors have been actively seeking reasons to sell, particularly targeting capital-light software businesses that may be vulnerable to AI disruption. However, he emphasized this is not merely about market jitters but reflects a genuine shift in how the market perceives AI’s threat to traditional business models.

Munster pointed to remarkable evidence of AI’s real-world impact on productivity, highlighting that Spotify’s (SPOT) CEO revealed the company’s best coders “have not written code since December.”

This development is significant because Spotify (SPOT) is not an AI-first company, yet its workforce is already transforming.

“The market is having a better sense that ultimately that this disruption is on the horizon,” Munster said.

The long-term value of software companies (IGPT), (XSW), (IGV) hinges on a fundamental question, according to Munster: whether agents will eventually outnumber humans in the workforce.

If bots replace human workers at scale, there will simply be less demand for traditional software seats, threatening the revenue models of giants like Salesforce (CRM) and Microsoft (MSFT). Munster expressed doubt that a company like Salesforce (CRM) could sustain a premium valuation “when you have that lingering out there.”

Munster shared that Deepwater is already experiencing this shift firsthand, using AI tools to build spreadsheets that bypass the need for traditional Microsoft licenses.

“Unless the software companies have a Google moment where they basically punch back like Google did with their search number back in the March,” he warned, “I think the case will still be open that bots are going to take seats lower.”

Without radical adjustments to hiring and infrastructure, Munster finds it difficult to imagine earnings continuing to grow for disrupted software firms. Until these companies demonstrate innovation that protects their core business or deliver exceptionally strong earnings showing no signs of disruption, their stock multiples will likely remain under pressure.

data.title: Small And Mid-Cap Semiconductor Stocks With Elevated P/S Ratios And Negative Ratings
data.description:
As AI disruption hits several sectors, below is a list of small- and mid-cap semiconductor stocks that carry Strong Sell or Sell recommendations under the Quant Rating system and are ranked by their price-to-sales (P/S) multiples.

The screen focuses on semiconductor companies within the small- and mid-cap range, filtering for names that combine negative quantitative ratings with elevated sales-based valuations.

The price-to-sales (P/S) multiple is a valuation metric that compares a company’s stock price to its revenue per share. It’s calculated by dividing a company’s market capitalization by its total revenue, or alternatively, by dividing its stock price by its revenue per share.

The list highlights semiconductor stocks that pair negative quantitative signals with P/S multiples relative to peers.

At the top are stocks with higher price-to-sales ratios, which may warrant caution given their unfavorable Quant Ratings.

The list is topped by Rigetti Computing, Inc. (RGTI), which has the highest price-to-sales multiple among the screened small- and mid-cap semiconductor names with a Strong Sell Quant Rating. Universal Display Corporation (OLED) and Impinj, Inc. (PI) follow, with Datavault AI Inc. (DVLT) and CEVA, Inc. (CEVA) rounding out the rest of the top five, all combining unfavorable quantitative scores with relatively high sales-based valuations.

Other notable names on the list, such as indie Semiconductor, Inc. (INDI) and Alpha and Omega Semiconductor Limited (AOSL), also feature on this screen alongside their Sell or Strong Sell Quant Ratings.

Here is the list:

  1. Rigetti Computing, Inc. (RGTI), Quant Rating: Strong Sell 1.30, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 567.63x

  2. Universal Display Corporation (OLED), Quant Rating: Sell 1.63, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 9.42x

  3. Impinj, Inc. (PI), Quant Rating: Strong Sell 1.19, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 8.95x

  4. Datavault AI Inc. (DVLT), Quant Rating: Strong Sell 1.26, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 6.93x

  5. CEVA, Inc. (CEVA), Quant Rating: Sell 1.57, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 4.99x

  6. indie Semiconductor, Inc. (INDI), Quant Rating: Sell 1.53, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 3.28x

  7. Alpha and Omega Semiconductor Limited (AOSL), Quant Rating: Strong Sell 1.42, price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 0.90x

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI)

data.title: Bullish chip stocks: A focus on small and mid caps ranked by price-to-sales multiple
data.description:
As we look at uncertain market conditions, below is a list of small- and mid-cap semiconductor stocks that carry Strong Buy or Buy recommendations under the Quant Rating system and are ranked by their price-to-sales (P/S) multiples.

The screen focuses on semiconductor companies within the small- and mid-cap range, filtering for names that combine favorable quantitative ratings with comparatively attractive sales-based valuations.

The price-to-sales (P/S) multiple is a valuation metric that compares a company’s stock price to its revenue per share. It’s calculated by dividing a company’s market capitalization by its total revenue, or alternatively, by dividing its stock price by its revenue per share.

The list is topped by Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NVTS), which has the highest price-to-sales multiple among the screened small- and mid-cap semiconductor names while still maintaining a Strong Buy Quant Rating. FormFactor, Inc. (FORM) and Silicon Laboratories Inc. (SLAB) follow, with Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA) and Semtech Corporation (SMTC) rounding out the rest of the top five, all combining favorable quantitative scores with their respective sales-based valuations.

Other notable names on the list, such as SkyWater Technology, Inc. (SKYT) and Cohu, Inc. (COHU), also feature competitive P/S multiples alongside supportive Quant Ratings, keeping them in focus for investors seeking semiconductor exposure at more moderate valuations.

Here is the list:

  1. Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NVTS), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 28.91x

  2. FormFactor, Inc. (FORM), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 9.23x

  3. Silicon Laboratories Inc. (SLAB), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 8.63x

  4. Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 7.47x

  5. Semtech Corporation (SMTC), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 7.21x

  6. SkyWater Technology, Inc. (SKYT), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 3.93x

  7. Cohu, Inc. (COHU), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 3.60x

  8. MaxLinear, Inc. (MXL), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 3.49x

  9. Synaptics Incorporated (SYNA), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 3.13x

  10. Diodes Incorporated (DIOD), price-to-sales (P/S) multiple: 2.25x

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI)

data.title: Ormat cut at Jefferies as upside from enhanced geothermal, data center already reflected
data.description:
Ormat Technologies (ORA) -1.8% in Friday's trading as Jefferies downgraded shares to Hold from Buy with a $130 price target, believing the stock already reflects much of the upside from enhanced geothermal systems and data centers, even when assuming a sharp ramp-up relative to historical MWs.

Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith said he remains bullish on Ormat's (ORA) long-term trajectory, with geothermal expansion supported by rising U.S. demand for baseload power, but he finds the stock's current valuation challenging as value increasingly shifts toward the development platform.

Ormat (ORA) shares enjoyed a strong run through 2025, but the analyst sees few near-term catalysts to drive incremental upside, particularly as enhanced geothermal system technology remains hypothetical and execution lags relative to 2028 capacity targets.

While additional data center contracts could drive shares higher in the coming months, Dumoulin-Smith thinks Ormat's (ORA) 250 MW data center opportunity already is reflected in current valuation.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Walmart earnings, FOMC minutes, major AI summit, and Chinese New Year
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - February 16

  • All week - The Chinese New Year holiday during the week could impact the consumer sector, including Las Vegas Sands (LVS), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Melco Resorts & Entertainment (MLCO), Trip.com (TCOM), Alibaba (BABA), JD.com (JD), PDD Holdings (PDD), and Yum China (YUMC).
  • All week - The Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference will take place in Orlando, Florida. Notable companies participating include Coca-Cola (KO), PepsiCo (PEP), General Mills (GIS), Kraft Heinz (KHC), Philip Morris (PM), and Celsius Holdings (CELH). The conference is known for company updates on long-term strategies and financial targets.
  • 8:25 a.m. Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman is scheduled to speak at the American Bankers Association Conference.

 

Tuesday - February 17

  • Volatility watch - Bitmine (BMNR) and Sandisk (SNDK) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include TechCreate Group (TCGL), Peakstone Realty Trust (PKST), and Ball (BALL). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Verisk (VRSK), Doximity (DOCS), and CS Disco (LAW).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Medtronic (MDT), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), Energy Transfer (ET), and Devon Energy (DVN). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Fluor (FLR) and Rush Street Interactive (RSI) after they report.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Chevron (CVX), ConocoPhillips (COP), Hasbro (HAS), and Microsoft (MSFT).
  • IPO watch - AGI (AGBK), Arko Petroleum (APC), and Clear Street Group (CLRS) are expected to price their IPOs and begin to trade. The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Bullish (BLSH), Nasus Pharma (NSRX), and Magnitude International (MAGH).
  • All day - Sonoco Products Company (SON) will hold an Investor Day event in New York City.
  • All day - 13F filings from hedge funds and investment managers on holdings at the end of Q4 are due to be disclosed by the end of the day.
  • 8:30 a.m. The U.S. Census Bureau is scheduled to release the January Retail Sales report.
  • 8:30 a.m. Radware (RDWR) will hold an Investor Day event in New York City.
  • 8:50 a.m. The three-day Barclays Industrial Select Conference will begin. Union Pacific (UNP), JetBlue Airways (JBLU), Jacobs (J), and Canadian Pacific Kansas City (CP) are some of the notable companies participating.

 

Wednesday - February 18

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Analog Devices (ADI), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Moody's (MCO), DoorDash (DASH), Blue Owl (OBDC), and Royal Gold (RGLD). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Carvana (CVNA) and Avis Budget (CAR) after they report.
  • All day - Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) will host an Analyst Day event in Boston. The event will feature presentations from Allegro’s executive leadership team on the company’s strategy and growth opportunities, product and technology differentiators, and target financial model.
  • All day - The 1-for-8 Noodles (NDLS) reverse stock split will be effective.
  • 8:30 a.m. The January Durable Goods order report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Housing Starts report is scheduled to be released by the U.S. Census Bureau and the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from its last meeting.

 

Thursday - February 19

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Walmart (WMT), Deere (DE), Newmont (NEM), Akamai Technologies (AKAM), Dropbox (DBX), and Consolidated Edison (ED). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Lemonade (LMND) and Etsy (ETSY) after they report.
  • All day - A10 Networks (ATEN) will host its Investor Day event in San Francisco. The leadership team will outline A10’s long-term growth framework across legacy networking, next-generation networking, and security.
  • All day - Meta Platforms (META) Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang will present at the India AI Impact Summit.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Philadelphia Fed Index report will be released.

 

Friday - February 20

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Lamar Advertising (LAMR).
  • All day - WTI crude March futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • All day - Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Bostic will give opening remarks at the Banking Conference Outlook.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for January will be released. Economists forecast a 2.7% increase.
  • All day - Qualcomm (QCOM) CEO Cristiano Amon will participate in a keynote session at the India AI Impact Summit.
  • All day - Anthropic (ANTHRO) CEO Dario Amodei will participate in a panel discussion at the India AI Impact Summit on public infrastructure for AI.
  • All day - It is the last trading day before the FDA action date on Medicenna Therapeutics' (MDNAF) Bizaxofusp.

data.title: ‘The broad economic picture is still inflationary’ – The Peterson Institute’s Adam Posen
data.description:
Adam Posen, president of the Peterson Institute for International Economics, warned that the broad economic outlook remains inflationary despite some positive signals in recent data.

“The broad picture is still inflationary,” Posen said in an interview with CNBC, cautioning investors against reading too much into minor fluctuations in monthly inflation reports.

Posen argued that getting “wound up about whether you missed on the upside or the downside expectations by a tenth of a point in a given month doesn’t give one very much information.”

He emphasized that the core services index continues to rise at a relatively high rate, suggesting underlying price pressures persist even as headline numbers show modest improvement.

The economist pointed to several concerning indicators beyond consumer prices, including a high Producer Price Index print that signals mounting costs at the supply level.

Posen noted that Federal Reserve beige books and media reporting indicate “companies getting ready to pass on more tariff hikes,” suggesting that current relief in core goods prices may prove temporary.

Anti-migration policies represent another inflationary risk, according to Posen. He warned that if migrants leave the workforce, “we’re going to start seeing that show up in shortages and price hikes in certain sectors like childcare.” The full impact of these policies on labor supply and prices remains uncertain.

Posen emphasized that fiscal policy decisions will play a critical role in determining the inflation trajectory, regardless of who leads the Federal Reserve. He noted that incoming Fed officials cannot control government spending, including potential checks to households or changes to healthcare subsidies that could sustain inflationary pressure.

While acknowledging that the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) has dropped recently, Posen suggested market participants may be underestimating structural risks.

He conceded his outlook could be wrong if fiscal expansion is blocked or migrants remain in the workforce, but maintained that the underlying economic momentum favors higher prices.

More on the U.S. economy

  • Headline and core CPI inflation rates cool Y/Y in January
  • U.S. economy overall is ‘weaker than widely anticipated’ - The Leuthold Group’s Jim Paulsen
  • Goldman Sachs’ Solomon: Economic landscape for 2026 is ‘quite good’ with a ‘constructive environment’
  • Markets will be looking for signs of tariff bleed in January CPI
  • Financial stress accelerates to record highs, says the National Foundation for Credit Counseling

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Stablecoins will become the default currency for AI agents
data.description:
Brian Armstrong, co-founder, chairman and CEO of Coinbase (COIN) said this week that he sees stablecoins continuing to grow, thanks to "unrivaled efficiency gains." In addition, he predicted that these digital assets would eventually become the go-to payment method for AI agents as they expand their use in the economy. 

"We're even seeing these AI agents adopt stablecoins for payment, and I believe that stablecoins will be the default payment method for AI agents," Armstrong said in the company's earnings conference call earlier this week.

"These AI agents that have been spinning up, we put out some really useful tools for developers to just get any AI agent a crypto wallet and begin to make stablecoin payments and begin to complete agentic commerce essentially," he added. "And that started to get quite a good amount of traction."

Armstrong is not the first crypto executive to see stablecoins as a good pairing with AI agents. Last month, Circle (CRCL) CEO Jeremy Allaire similarly predicted that the digital assets could become central to the AI agent economy.

COIN jumped 18% on Friday following the release of its quarterly results. The headline figures missed expectations, but investors scooped up the beaten-down stock on optimism over revenue diversification and the legislative outlook.

Overall, COIN has dropped 26% so far this year, part of a general fall in the crypto industry. CRCL is down 24% in 2026, while Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has slumped 22%. Stocks like Strategy (MSTR), MARA Holdings (MARA) and Bit Digital (BTBT) are all down by double digits as well.

data.title: Reddit soars over 6% after director buys shares worth $7.48M
data.description:
Reddit (RDDT) shares surged over 6% on Friday afternoon after director Sarah Farrell purchased company shares worth $7.48M.

Farrell bought a total of 50,500 shares across two transactions between February 10 and February 11. The sales were executed in multiple trades at prices ranging from $139.27 to $149.79.

Following the purchase, her holdings have now increased to 58,399 shares.

Earlier in the week, Reddit’s CFO, Christopher Slowe, also made a sizeable transaction where he divested 11,464 shares for a total value of $1.72M. This sale, however, was made under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, which permits insiders of publicly listed companies to sell a set number of shares at a prearranged time and price, helping safeguard against insider trading concerns.

data.title: Xcel Energy upgraded to Buy, Edison International cut at UBS
data.description:
Xcel Energy (XEL) +2.9% in Friday's trading to a YTD high as UBS upgraded shares to Buy from Neutral with an $89 price target, raised from $81, which estimates the stock is pricing in sub-7% EPS growth, a full 200 bps below the bank's forecasts.

UBS analyst William Apicelli said Xcel's (XEL) high-quality, fully regulated 9% EPS growth rate and increasing data center load growth trends look to be unrecognized by investors, with the stock trading at a depressed 4% discount to the broader sector.

Xcel (XEL) raised the data center high probability load upside to 4 GW from 2 GW on its recent year-end call and cited increasing investment opportunities from SPP transmission and state resource plans led by 2.1 GW in Colorado, while resolution of the legacy Smokehouse fire liability in Texas likely will be a small outlay of less than $100M, the analyst noted.

At the same time, UBS downgraded Edison International (EIX) to Neutral from Buy with a $78 PT, lowered from $70, following the stock's strong performance over the past six months.

While Edison (EIX) shares remain discounted relative to a potential Eaton fire liability resolution, any potential catalyst from Phase 2 wildfire legislation likely is six months away, limiting near-term upside potential, and the upcoming California gubernatorial election introduces policy and regulatory risk related to affordability concerns.

data.title: Sixth Street Specialty Lending targets 11–11.5% ROE for 2026 as it expands investment flexibility with new joint venture
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sixth Street Specialty Lending, Inc. (TSLX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Stanley opened his first earnings call as CEO by highlighting the platform's continued strength, discipline amid a dynamic environment, and the appointment of Ross Bruck as Managing Director and Head of Investment Strategy. Stanley stated, “Ross brings a unique perspective that bridges complex asset level underwriting with a strategic lens on market opportunity. His appointment reflects our commitment to elevating our internal talent to drive disciplined investment decisions.”
  • Stanley reported adjusted net investment income of $0.52 per share and adjusted net income of $0.30 per share for the quarter, with annualized operating ROE of 12% and net income ROE of 7%. He emphasized, “Adjusted net investment income of $0.52 per share exceeded our base dividend of $0.46 per share, providing base dividend coverage of 113%.”
  • The company continued its focus on the durability of business models, especially in enterprise software and AI impacts, noting, “We are taking a forward-looking approach in how we underwrite and manage risk. Long-time followers will know that our team has been investing in technology-related businesses for more than 2 decades, and we've navigated multiple periods of significant change.”
  • Stanley described the core portfolio as demonstrating strong credit statistics with consistent revenue and EBITDA growth, and noted a stable weighted average LTV of approximately 41%. He also disclosed that enterprise software exposure is about 40% of the total portfolio by fair value.
  • The Board approved a base quarterly dividend of $0.46 per share and a supplemental dividend of $0.01 per share for Q4.
  • Ross Bruck noted, “Our base case is that the investment environment for 2026 will be characterized by the continued imbalance between the supply of private capital and the demand for financing, resulting in sustained levels of competition and tight spreads for regular way on-the-run transactions.”
  • Bruck reported $242.4 million in commitments and $196.7 million in fundings in Q4, with 97% of investments in First Lien loans and 5 new investments across 4 industries.
  • CFO Ian Simmonds commented, “In Q4, we generated net investment income per share of $0.53, resulting in full year net investment income per share of $2.23.”
  • Simmonds highlighted the formation of Structured Credit Partners (SCP), a joint venture with Carlyle Group BDCs: “We believe SCP will generate returns in the mid-teens on capital invested, which will be accretive to our overall asset level yields. SLX's total commitment to the joint venture is $200 million.”

Outlook

  • Simmonds stated, “Based on our model, which incorporates the forward curve reflects leverage in the middle of our target range and assumes spreads on new investments remain broadly stable, we expect to target a return on equity on net investment income for 2026 of 11% to 11.5%.”
  • The company expects adjusted net investment income per share for 2026 to be in the range of $1.87 to $1.95 using year-end book value per share of $16.97, with $1.21 per share of spillover income at year-end.
  • Management reiterated confidence in dividend coverage and the earnings power of the portfolio, with the anticipated JV returns providing additional support.

Financial Results

  • Net asset value per share at year-end was $16.98, compared to $17.11 in Q3 and $17.09 at year-end 2024 after supplemental dividends.
  • Total investments were $3.3 billion, principal debt outstanding was $1.8 billion, and net assets were $1.6 billion.
  • Debt-to-equity ratio at year-end was 1.1x, down from 1.15x in the prior quarter; average debt-to-equity ratio increased to 1.17x.
  • Total investment income for Q4 was $108.2 million, with interest and dividend income of $95.5 million and other fees of $10.9 million.
  • Weighted average yield on debt and income-producing securities at amortized cost decreased from 11.7% to 11.3% quarter-over-quarter.
  • Non-accruals remained unchanged at 0.6% by fair value.

Q&A

  • Brian McKenna, Citizens JMP Securities: Asked about portfolio turnover since 2022 and the evolution of deployment opportunities around AI. Stanley responded that about 20–25% of NAV is pre-2022 vintages, with most of the portfolio originated post rate hiking cycle. He explained the thematic approach: “Themes rotate often. 18 to 24 months is the general gestation period of a theme, and we're constantly rotating across the platform...those are the ones that I talked about that have the strong moats.”
  • Finian O'Shea, Wells Fargo Securities: Inquired about the SCP JV structure and spillover income implications. Bruck clarified, “These will be broadly syndicated loan CLOs. We don't anticipate the CLOs holding private credit either originated by us or third parties.” Simmonds addressed spillover, noting the ramp-up will be gradual and the framework will help manage stability in NAV.
  • Arren Cyganovich, Truist Securities: Asked about the investment pipeline and unrealized losses. Stanley said, “I think the pipeline is decent. We had good activity in Q4 as you saw a pickup in M&A activity.” Simmonds detailed the losses were primarily due to spread movements and specific reversals.
  • Kenneth Lee, RBC Capital Markets: Sought more on SCP motivations and spread dynamics. Bruck said, “We think the risk return generated by fee-free CLO equity is really attractive within a portfolio context for SLX.” Stanley noted spreads have been stable and does not anticipate major changes soon.
  • Paul Johnson, KBW: Queried the 40% software exposure trend. Stanley stated the exposure “has come down marginally over the past couple of years, in part because we were seeing a decline in unit economics across the software space post the COVID pull-through of demand.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of spreads, impact of AI on portfolio durability, pipeline evolution, SCP JV implications, and the company’s ability to capitalize on dislocations. The tone was neutral, with probing on new ventures and risk management but no overt skepticism.
  • Management's tone during prepared remarks was confident and disciplined, emphasizing risk management, thematic investing, and flexibility. During Q&A, the tone remained steady and constructive, with Stanley and Simmonds providing detailed explanations without defensive language.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from transition and succession planning to a focus on strategy execution and forward-looking risk assessment, while analysts’ tone remained neutral and consistently focused on operational details and strategic moves.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance was introduced for 2026 targeting 11–11.5% ROE, while the prior quarter focused on 2025 performance and did not give explicit 2026 targets.
  • Strategic focus expanded to include the SCP JV with Carlyle, aimed at enhancing investment flexibility and returns, whereas the previous call discussed CLO liabilities as a tactical deployment of capital.
  • Analysts’ questions this quarter centered more on new ventures (SCP JV), AI impacts, and the detailed composition of software exposure, while the previous quarter focused heavily on leadership transitions and capital allocation philosophy.
  • Key metrics such as adjusted net investment income per share and NAV per share remained stable quarter-over-quarter, with a slight decrease in NAV and a modest decline in total investment income.
  • Management’s confidence in navigating market volatility and leveraging thematic investing was reinforced, contrasting with the prior quarter’s emphasis on continuity and transition.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited sustained competition, tight spreads, and macroeconomic uncertainty as ongoing challenges.
  • The SCP JV’s ramp-up and its impact on spillover income will be monitored to avoid NAV instability.
  • Exposure to enterprise software and the potential for AI-driven disruption were addressed, with management stressing a focus on durable business models and maintaining conservative lending standards.
  • Analysts raised concerns about unrealized losses, sector dislocations, and spread sustainability, which management addressed by detailing risk management frameworks and portfolio positioning.

Final Takeaway

Sixth Street Specialty Lending enters 2026 with a clear focus on disciplined asset selection, robust liquidity, and strategic flexibility, highlighted by the launch of a major joint venture and a targeted ROE of 11–11.5%. With a strong portfolio foundation, prudent risk management, and a forward-looking approach to market opportunities—particularly in technology and structured credit—the company signals confidence in its ability to sustain earnings, cover dividends, and capitalize on evolving credit market dynamics.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
